teamObjective.explanation: Leading the Ace maximizes the chance of winning the first trick and following with the 2 to go out first. The 2 lead cedes initiative and makes my exit dependent on later mistakes by others.

partnerBelief.summary: Partner (player 2, 3 cards, team 0) has no public actions yet. With me at only 2 cards, partner's most valuable signal is implicit support for an endgame race: they will try to win tricks or pass control back so I can shed my remaining cards and go out first.

opponentBelief.summary: Opponents (3 cards each) know I am one trick away from emptying my hand. They will try to seize control, beat my leads, and force me to waste my high card; they may hold trump-rank 7s specifically to block my exit.

actionRationale.primaryReason: Leading the Ace maximizes the chance of winning the first trick and following with the 2 to go out first. The 2 lead cedes initiative and makes my exit dependent on later mistakes by others.

whyNotAlternatives.reason: Legal alternative retained for verifier comparison after schema repair.

risks: Schema repair may preserve imperfect reasoning from the raw model output.
riskAssessment.mitigation: Treat the repaired trace as schema-normalized reasoning; do not add hidden-card facts beyond the raw output.

notes: Deterministically schema-repaired from a ToM-prompted raw output; selectedActionId is unchanged.
